content.verdict: The Hyatt Regency Aqaba Ayla is Jordan's flagship Red Sea beach resort, wrapped around the new Ayla Oasis marina with views across the Gulf of Aqaba to Egypt, Israel and Saudi Arabia all at once. Honeymooners get warm-water diving, coral reefs and proper beach days — the perfect counterpoint to Petra and the desert.
content.best_room: The Regency Suite with sea view and balcony looks directly across the Red Sea to the lights of Eilat and the mountains of Sinai. For honeymooners wanting space, the Presidential Suite offers 180 square metres, a dining area, and the best sunset balcony in Aqaba facing due west over the water.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Red Sea arrival
itinerary_7_nights.description: Fly into King Hussein International Airport (AQJ), a 10-minute transfer to the resort. Check in, stroll the marina boardwalk and watch the sun set over Sinai from your balcony before dinner at Levantine restaurant Al Sufra.

itinerary_7_nights.title: First dive or snorkel
itinerary_7_nights.description: Half-day with the on-site PADI dive centre to the Cedar Pride wreck or the Japanese Garden reef — both shore-accessible. Afternoon at the infinity pool and sunset drinks at the marina.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Wadi Rum day trip
itinerary_7_nights.description: One-hour drive inland to Wadi Rum for a 4x4 desert tour, lunch in a Bedouin camp and a traditional zarb sand oven dinner. Return to Aqaba for a late-night dip in the pool under the stars.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Glass-boat and beach
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning glass-bottom boat trip across the Gulf to see reefs without getting wet, followed by a beach picnic on the resort's private stretch and a couples treatment at Shakhat Spa.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Petra overnight option
itinerary_7_nights.description: Two-hour drive north to Petra for a guided Siq-to-Treasury visit and overnight at a Petra hotel, or a long day trip returning to Aqaba for cocktails at Sunset Terrace.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Sailing sunset cruise
itinerary_7_nights.description: Charter a private catamaran from the Ayla marina for a sunset cruise across the Gulf, swimming stops at remote reefs and canapés on deck as the call to prayer echoes across four countries.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Farewell
itinerary_7_nights.description: A final dive or beach morning, late brunch at the pool and transfer the short distance back to AQJ for the flight home or onward leg via Amman.

caveats: Aqaba is a working port city with occasional cargo ship traffic visible from the south-facing rooms, so specifically request a north or west-facing suite for the postcard Sinai sunset view.
Summer Red Sea water temperatures reach 29C which is glorious for swimming but can cause coral bleaching stress — brief your dive guide to choose the deeper, cooler reef sites in July and August.
The resort is brand new within the master-planned Ayla Oasis community which is still under construction in places, so some surrounding villas and restaurants may still be opening during your stay.

faqs.question: Is this hotel good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Yes — the Red Sea setting, on-site dive centre, long private beach and Ayla marina combine for a strong beach-honeymoon week. Book a sea-view suite and combine with Petra and Wadi Rum for a complete Jordan honeymoon.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: March to May and September to November offer 25-30C air and perfect 24-26C sea temperatures for diving. December to February is still swimmable at around 22C and gives you near-empty beaches.

faqs.question: What should we book in advance?
faqs.answer: Reserve a sea-view Regency or Presidential Suite, a couples treatment at Shakhat Spa, a PADI discover-scuba session and a Wadi Rum overnight or day trip through the concierge.

faqs.question: Is it adults-only?
faqs.answer: No, the resort is family-friendly with a kids club, but the size of the property and zoning of pools means honeymooners can easily find adult corners. Book a higher floor away from the family pool zone.

faqs.question: What's the best room type for a honeymoon?
faqs.answer: A Regency Suite with sea view balcony is the honeymoon sweet spot, pairing a king bed, living area and direct Sinai sunset view. The Presidential Suite is the ultimate splurge for a special anniversary.

faqs.question: How do we get there?
faqs.answer: Fly into King Hussein International Airport (AQJ) in Aqaba for a 10-minute transfer, or into Amman (AMM) and either connect domestically in 50 minutes or drive four hours south via the Desert Highway.
